purpose: Targeted brush-up registry for agents. AGENTS.md remains the common denominator; skills carry conditional lane knowledge backed by executable contracts and current repo evidence.
authorityRules: Skills explain executable contracts, policies, manifests, source files, and current docs; they do not override them.
Load only the narrowest skill needed for the active lane.
Stale PRs are classified and selectively replayed; they are not blindly merged, squashed, deleted, or used as general bases.
Harness maturity is raised by moving cross-cutting plumbing into contracts, policies, registries, guards, evidence, and reporting; do not move domain behavior into harness just to chase a percentage.
Long chat annotations and stale snapshots must be preserved as classified insight, not treated as current truth until verified.

useWhen: mapping PRs, branches, worktrees, conflicts, dirty state, stale generated artifacts, or safe bases
creating a sprint map before feature work
editing AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill files, agent prompts, harness context docs, or skill manifests
deciding what belongs in common agent rules versus targeted skills
deciding whether logic belongs in harness plumbing, skill/domain code, workflow contracts, policy guards, registries, or evidence/reporting layers
reviewing a proposed refactor that claims to make the app more harness-driven
doNotUseFor: moving behavior solely to increase a harness percentage
claiming runtime proof from static architecture changes

useWhen: preserving high-value chat annotations, stale snapshots, PR comments, and runtime lessons without losing provenance
choosing the most rewarding next sprint across repo hygiene, harness maturity, runtime proof automation, evidence retention, and agent operations
doNotUseFor: treating stale snapshots as current truth without verification
closing PRs or deleting evidence without replacement or archive rationale
claiming runtime proof from static documentation

useWhen: recovering useful work from stale, conflicted, superseded, or stacked PRs
deciding whether to replay, replace, close, or preserve an old branch
doNotUseFor: using stale PR heads as general bases
